headline: 1MDB Asset Recovery Has Reached Over RM20.7 Billion
context: The Ministry of Finance reported in October 2025 that Malaysia had recovered more than RM20.73 billion through settlements with financial institutions involved in the 1MDB scandal, substantially more than earlier official tallies suggested.

cards.text: 1MDB asset recovery has surpassed RM20.7 billion. A decade in, the headline number is now bigger than most people remember.
cards.sub: MOF figure as of October 2025. The Goldman Sachs 2020 settlement alone returned USD3.9 billion to Malaysia.

cards.h: Goldman Sachs paid USD3.9 billion in 2020, the largest single 1MDB-related recovery to date.
cards.s: The settlement included a USD2.5 billion cash payment plus a guarantee to help recover at least USD1.4 billion in misappropriated assets.
cards.d: Goldman Sachs paid USD3.9 billion in 2020, the largest single 1MDB-related recovery to date. The settlement included a USD2.5 billion cash payment plus a guarantee to help recover at least USD1.4 billion in misappropriated assets.

cards.h: Recovery flows from settlements with financial institutions, asset seizures across multiple jurisdictions, and criminal prosecutions.
cards.s: The US Department of Justice has separately recovered and returned about USD1.4 billion in assets to Malaysia by mid-2024.
cards.d: Recovery flows from settlements with financial institutions, asset seizures across multiple jurisdictions, and criminal prosecutions. The US Department of Justice has separately recovered and returned about USD1.4 billion in assets to Malaysia by mid-2024.

cards.h: Total scandal losses remain disputed. Figures cited range from USD4.5 billion (DOJ) up to RM30+ billion in broader Malaysian estimates.
cards.s: Recovery comparisons depend on which loss baseline is used. Multiple jurisdictions have ongoing litigation that may produce further recoveries.
cards.d: Total scandal losses remain disputed. Figures cited range from USD4.5 billion (DOJ) up to RM30+ billion in broader Malaysian estimates. Recovery comparisons depend on which loss baseline is used. Multiple jurisdictions have ongoing litigation that may produce further recoveries.

cards.text: The recovery story is no longer 'how little came back'. It is whether the recovered funds reach public accounts and reform follows.
RM20.7 billion is the largest financial-crime recovery in Malaysian history. The remaining test is whether the institutions that enabled the scandal have been structurally changed.

confidence: Low — derived from published cards, original brief not on disk; submit for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
